Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Try Case Within Five Years And Six Months; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
DONALD H. MEDEARIS, Esq., BAY AREA LEGAL AID (415) 354-6331 dmedearis@baylegal.org
Attorneys for Petitioner, HADAS GERGI
DANE C. BARCA. Esq., Deputy Attorney General (415) 510-3371 Dane.Barca@doj.ca.gov Attorneys for Respondents
Recorded by "For The Record"

Respondent contests the Court's tentative ruling.

Hearing is held. Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT PAT LEARY , IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS ACTING DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Try Case Within Five Years And Six Months

Respondent California Department of Social Services (CDSS)'s motion to dismiss petitioner Hadas Gergis' petition for writ of mandate is denied. (see word minutes for complete details)

Counsel for Plainitff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling as modified. Compliance with CRC 3.1312 is required.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Recorded by FOR THE RECORD (FTR) (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Try Case Within Five Years And Six Months; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-05-2025 Continued To Mar-04-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set A Hearing Date For Petitioner'S Ccp 1094.5 Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Feb-04-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. DONALD MEDEARIS, Esq. of Bay Area Legal Aid (tel: 415-354-6331) for Petitioner.
Atty. DANE BARCA, Esq. of the Office of the Attorney General (tel: 415-510-3371) for Respondents.

Hearing Re: PETITIONER HADAS GERGIS' Motion To Set A Hearing Date For Petitioner'S Ccp 1094.5 Writ Of Administrative Mandate.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner's motion to set a hearing date for the writ of administrative mandamus is denied. The court determines that defendant's motion to dismiss per CCP 583.310 and 583.360 (including any tolling issues), should be resolved before the court sets the hearing on the writ for administrative mandamus. If the court dismisses the action, then the court and respondent will not need to exert resources regarding the underlying merits of the dispute. If the court denies the motion to dismiss, then the court will set a merits-briefing schedule, which can be discussed at the hearing on the motion to dismiss on March 4, 2025.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter outlining compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Reported By For The Record. (301/CVA)
